docs.title: The Cybersecurity Archive
docs.text:
A structured, no-nonsense knowledge base covering cybersecurity from the ground up. Built for learners who want depth, not just surface-level overviews.

New here?

Start with 00 · Introduction and follow the modules in order. If you already have a foundation, jump to whatever domain you're focusing on.

docs.title: Foundations
docs.text: Module Topics 00 · Introduction CIA Triad, Threat Modeling, Zero Trust, Defense in Depth, Attacker Mindset 01 · Security Foundations OS Security, Linux, Windows, Authentication, Authorization, Identity 02 · Network Security OSI Model, TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/S, TLS, Firewalls, VPNs, Network Attacks 03 · Cryptography Symmetric & Asymmetric Encryption, Hashing, PKI, Key Management

docs.title: Attack & Defense
docs.text: Module Topics 04 · Web Security XSS, SQLi, CSRF, SSRF, RCE, CORS, Auth Attacks 05 · Application Security Secure Coding, SAST, DAST, Dependency Security, Secure SDLC 06 · Offensive Security Recon, Enumeration, Exploitation, Privilege Escalation, Persistence 07 · Pentesting Web, Network, Wireless, Active Directory, Cloud Pentesting, Reporting 08 · Defensive Security Monitoring, Threat Detection, Log Analysis, Threat Hunting, Triage

docs.title: Specialized Domains
docs.text: Module Topics 09 · Malware Analysis Malware Types, Static & Dynamic Analysis, Reverse Engineering 10 · DFIR Disk & Memory Forensics, Evidence Collection, Incident Response 11 · Cloud Security IAM, Misconfigurations, Containers, Kubernetes, Serverless 12 · Identity & Access Passwords, MFA, SSO, OAuth, LDAP, Kerberos 13 · Endpoint Security AV, EDR, Process Monitoring, Kernel Security 14 · Mobile Security Android, iOS, APK Analysis, Mobile Pentesting 15 · Wireless Security WiFi, Bluetooth, NFC, Rogue APs 16 · IoT & Embedded Firmware Analysis, UART, JTAG, Hardware Debugging

docs.title: Strategy & Operations
docs.text: Module Topics 17 · Threat Intelligence IOCs, TTPs, Threat Actors, MITRE ATT&CK 18 · GRC Risk Assessment, Policies, Compliance, Auditing, Controls 19 · Privacy & Data Protection Data Classification, Privacy Engineering, Consent, Retention 20 · Security Automation Python, Bash, PowerShell, SOAR, Detection Engineering 21 · Vulnerability Research Fuzzing, Exploit Dev, Binary Exploitation, Memory Corruption 22 · Human Security Phishing, Social Engineering, Insider Threats, Awareness

docs.title: Career
docs.text: Module Topics 23 · Career & Industry Certifications, Labs, Bug Bounties, Research, Career Paths 24 · Resources Tools, Labs, Cheat Sheets, Glossaries, References

docs.title: Suggested Learning Paths
docs.text:
Complete BeginnerAspiring Red TeamerAspiring Blue TeamerCloud SecurityGRC / Compliance 
00 Introduction → 01 Security Foundations → 02 Network Security
→ 03 Cryptography → 04 Web Security → 23 Career & Industry

00 Introduction → 02 Network Security → 04 Web Security
→ 06 Offensive Security → 07 Pentesting → 21 Vulnerability Research

00 Introduction → 01 Security Foundations → 08 Defensive Security
→ 09 Malware Analysis → 10 DFIR → 17 Threat Intelligence

00 Introduction → 02 Network Security → 11 Cloud Security
→ 12 Identity & Access → 18 GRC → 20 Security Automation

00 Introduction → 18 GRC → 19 Privacy & Data Protection
→ 17 Threat Intelligence → 23 Career & Industry

This archive is actively maintained. Contributions welcome via GitHub.

docs.title: 00 · Introduction
docs.text: This module is your entry point into cybersecurity. Before tools, before techniques, before certifications — you need the mental model. That's what this module builds.

docs.title: What's in This Module
docs.text: # Topic What You'll Learn 1 What is Cybersecurity Scope of the field, why it matters, how it's structured 2 Types of Hackers White hat, black hat, grey hat, nation-states, hacktivists 3 Attacker Mindset How attackers think, why offense informs defense 4 Vulnerability vs Threat vs Risk Core vocabulary, often confused — get it right from day one 5 Attack Types Overview Active vs passive attacks, targeted vs opportunistic 6 CIA Triad Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability — the foundation 7 Security Principles Least privilege, fail-safe defaults, separation of duties 8 Threat Modeling STRIDE, attack trees, how to think about threats systematically 9 Defense in Depth Layered security, why no single control is enough 10 Zero Trust Never trust, always verify — the modern security model 11 Security Domains Overview Map of the entire field — where everything connects 12 How to Use This Repo Learning paths, how content is structured, what to do next

docs.title: Learning Order
docs.text:
Follow the table above top to bottom. Each topic builds on the previous one. Don't skip ahead — the frameworks in topics 6–10 only make sense after the vocabulary in 1–5.

Time estimate

Plan for 6–10 hours to work through this module properly. Reading fast is not the goal. Understanding is.
